verifier_packages.description: Passes when target contains the Base64 encoding of source bytes.
verifier_packages.verify_py:
import base64
import json
import sys

request = json.load(sys.stdin)
verifier_id = request["verifier"]["id"]
inputs = request.get("inputs") or {}

def fail(code, message):
    print(json.dumps({"passed": False, "error": {"code": code, "message": message}}))
    sys.exit(0)

def passed(content):
    print(json.dumps({"passed": True, "evidence": [{"id": verifier_id, "type": verifier_id, "content": content}], "outputs": content}))
    sys.exit(0)

source = inputs.get("source")
target = inputs.get("target")
if not source or not target:
    fail("missing_input", "source and target are required")
with open(source, "rb") as handle:
    expected = base64.b64encode(handle.read()).decode("ascii")
with open(target, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as handle:
    actual = "".join(handle.read().split())
if actual != expected:
    fail("base64_mismatch", "target is not the Base64 encoding of source")
passed({"source": source, "target": target})

candidates.description: Encode source bytes as Base64 text.
